claim: Electromagnetic interference has no effect on Radio Frequency Identification (RFID) readability and reading distances.

result.claim: Electromagnetic interference has no effect on Radio Frequency Identification (RFID) readability and reading distances.
result.reasoning: The claim that electromagnetic interference has no effect on RFID readability and reading distances is refuted by evidence. Paper ID 15251067, a review with a credibility rating of 2.5/5, explicitly states that "Electromagnetic interference (EMI) promises to be an ever-evolving concern for flight electronic systems" and "identifies its impact upon civil aviation radio systems," specifically mentioning "radio frequency identification (RFID)" as one of the new wireless services being introduced into passenger airplanes that are subject to this impact. This directly indicates that EMI *does* have an effect on RFID systems, contradicting the claim.

evidence.text: Interhemispheric transfer latency was minimized under gamma band.
evidence.source: 25th Annual Computational Neuroscience Meeting: CNS-2016.

evidence.text: Future scanners will use higher frequencies in the terahertz range.
evidence.source: Terahertz Electromagnetic Fields (0.106 THz) Do Not Induce Manifest Genomic Damage In Vitro

evidence.text: The BioTek microplate reader used an excitation wavelength of 502 nm.
evidence.source: Inhibitory Effects of Syringic Acid on Endometrial Cancer Cell Growth and Migration and Its Synergistic Suppression with Doxorubicin.

evidence.text: Every second gamma peak was suppressed, resulting in a decrease in gamma power.
evidence.source: 25th Annual Computational Neuroscience Meeting: CNS-2016.

evidence.text: The Frey effect is also termed the microwave auditory effect, then radio-frequency hearing.
evidence.source: ‘Sonic Attacks’ on U.S. Diplomats in Cuba: Auditory Dysfunction Remains Unsolved Mystery

evidence.text: ORAI1 channel distance was decreased from 47 to 37.7 nm in the simulations.

evidence.text: LBR and LB1 levels were reduced after irradiation.
